content.problemStatement:
There are three **distinct** points - A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C in the X−YX-YX−Y plane. Initially, you are located at point AAA. You want to reach the point CCC satisfying the following conditions:


You have to go through point B.
You can move in any of the four axis-parallel directions (+X+X+X, −X-X−X, +Y+Y+Y, −Y-Y−Y direction). However, you can make **at most** one turn in the path from AAA to CCC.
Determine if it is possible to reach the destination CCC satisfying the above conditions.

**NOTE**:


One turn means 90∘90 ^ \circ90∘  clockwise or anticlockwise rotation along the direction of movement. For example, if you are moving towards the +X+X+X direction, after one turn you can move either in the +Y+Y+Y direction or in the −Y-Y−Y direction. **Changing the direction by 180∘180 ^ \circ180∘ is not allowed**.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XA,YAX_A, Y_AXA​,YA​, denoting the coordinates of the starting point AAA.
The second line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XB,YBX_B, Y_BXB​,YB​, denoting the coordinates of the point BBB.
The third line of each test case contains two space-separated integers XC,YCX_C, Y_CXC​,YC​, denoting the coordinates of the finishing point CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one string - `"YES"`(without quotes) if it is possible to reach the finishing point satisfying the above conditions, otherwise print `"NO"`(without quotes).
constraints: -10^9 <= XA, YA, XB, YB, XC, YC <= 10^9
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, three lines follow:
- Line 1: two integers XA YA – coordinates of point A.
- Line 2: two integers XB YB – coordinates of point B.
- Line 3: two integers XC YC – coordinates of point C.
All coordinates are between -10^9 and 10^9 and the three points are pairwise distinct.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to travel from A to C passing through B with at most one 90° turn using only axis‑parallel moves, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: A(1,1)->B(1,3) vertical, turn, B->C(3,3) horizontal – one turn, YES.
Test case 2: No way with at most one turn – NO.
Test case 3: A(5,2)->C(1,2) straight horizontal passing through B(3,2) – no turn, YES.
Test case 4: Any path needs two turns – NO.

First case: points are diagonal, cannot move without turning more than once – NO.
Second case: vertical A->B then horizontal B->C – one turn – YES.

solution.approach:
The whole path consists of at most two straight axis‑parallel segments: A→B and B→C. Therefore:
1. A and B must share either the same x‑coordinate (vertical segment) or the same y‑coordinate (horizontal segment).
2. B and C must also share either the same x‑coordinate or the same y‑coordinate.
3. If both segments are vertical (A.x == B.x == C.x) or both are horizontal (A.y == B.y == C.y) we are moving along a straight line. This is allowed only when B lies between A and C, i.e. the coordinate of B is between the coordinates of A and C.
4. If one segment is vertical and the other horizontal we make exactly one 90° turn, which is always allowed.
5. The case where the direction would reverse (180° turn) is excluded by condition 3 because then B would not be between A and C.
The algorithm checks these conditions in O(1) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
